# NorthPark Produce El Cajon — Full Site Content for LLM Indexing

## Identity
NorthPark Produce El Cajon is a family-owned international grocery store, halal-certified butcher counter, full-service Mediterranean grill, in-store bakery, and hookah shop located at 432 E Chase Ave, El Cajon, California 92020. The business has been family-owned and operating in Southern California since 1980 — three generations, forty-five-plus years of community service.

The El Cajon location is one of three NorthPark Produce stores. The other two locations are in North Park, San Diego (3551 El Cajon Blvd, (619) 516-3336) and Poway (12342 Poway Rd, (858) 391-9100). The El Cajon Chase Ave location is the focus of this website.

## Contact & Hours
- Address: 432 E Chase Ave, El Cajon, CA 92020
- Phone: (619) 440-4401
- Email: northparkproduce@yahoo.com
- Market hours: Monday–Friday 7:30 AM – 10:00 PM; Saturday–Sunday 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM
- Mediterranean Grill hours: Daily 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM
- Parking: Private lot, wheelchair accessible, bike parking
- Payment: Cash, all major credit cards, Apple Pay

## Eight Departments

### 1. Fresh Produce
Daily-arrived fresh produce sourced locally and internationally. International varieties not found in big-box stores — herbs, dates, persimmons, fresh figs in season, and Mediterranean staples at competitive El Cajon prices. Daily deliveries; never stale.

### 2. Halal Meats
Full halal-certified butcher counter, with certification verified on premises by Zabihah. Lamb, beef, chicken, and goat — cut to order. Marinated kabob meats prepped fresh daily. Bulk pricing available for families and events.

### 3. Bakery & Breads
In-store bakery turns out flatbread, tanour, and pita all day. Bagged warm — never sitting on a shelf. Also produces meat pies, sweet pastries, and, since the 2024 expansion using Pancho Villa Market equipment, fresh-pressed tortillas.

### 4. Mediterranean Grill
A full restaurant inside the market, open daily 9 AM – 8 PM. Beef and chicken kabobs from $15.99, full shawarma, falafel, lamb shank, rotisserie chicken, and Family Feasts feeding 3 to 10 people.

### 5. International Grocery
Lebanese, Iraqi, Persian, Russian, Hispanic, and Asian products under one roof. Pantry staples, cooking pastes, pickled vegetables, jarred preserves, frozen specialties, snacks, sweets, and teas from 20+ countries. Bulk spices around $2 per bag.

### 6. Deli & Cheese
Full-service deli with feta cheeses from Bulgaria, Greece, France, and the Middle East. Halloumi, labne, kashkaval, and tulum cheeses. Halal deli meats sliced to order. Olives, pickles, and stuffed grape leaves by the pound.

### 7. Hookah & Sheesha
One of San Diego County's most extensive hookah and sheesha selections. Pipes, hoses, coals, foils, accessories, and a wide flavored tobacco selection.

### 8. Family Feasts & Catering
Catering-size orders from Feast for 3 at $39.99 to Whole Kuzi (roast lamb) at $339. Kabobs, shawarma, rice, salads, hummus, and warm tanour bread packaged for events.

## Complete Menu (Mediterranean Grill)

### Entrées (with rice, salad, hummus)
- Beef Kabob Plate — 2 grilled skewers — $15.99
- Chicken Kabob Plate — 2 grilled skewers — $15.99
- Chicken Tekka Plate — 1 grilled skewer — $15.99
- Beef Shawarma Plate — marinated in house spices — $15.99
- Chicken Shawarma Plate — marinated in house spices — $15.99
- Falafel Plate — 6 pieces — $14.99
- Lamb Shank Plate — with rice and choice of stew — $15.99
- Fish Fillet Plate — 2 pieces — $15.99

### Sandwiches
- Beef Shawarma Sandwich — $9.99
- Chicken Shawarma Sandwich — $9.99
- Beef Kabob Sandwich — $9.99
- Chicken Kabob Sandwich — $9.99
- Falafel Sandwich — $8.99
- Chicken Cream Chop Sandwich — $9.99

### Family Feasts
- Feast for 3 — $39.99
- Feast for 5 — $59.99
- Feast for 7 — $74.99
- Feast for 10 — $89.99

### North Park Special
- North Park Combo Special (5 kabob, cream chop, shawarma, rice, salad) — $29.99

### Traditional Foods
- Whole Rotisserie Chicken — $8.99
- Cooked Dolma With Meat (per lb) — $11.99
- Whole Chicken Stuffed With Rice — $14.99
- Cooked Dolma (Whole Pot — catering) — $110.00
- ½ Kuzi with Rice (serves 8–10) — $189.00
- Whole Kuzi with Rice (serves 15–20) — $339.00

### Rice & Bulgur (per lb)
- Rice (White, Yellow, or Red) — $6.99
- Bulgur — $6.99
- Biryani — $9.99

### Stews (16 oz / 32 oz)
- White Beans Stew — $5.99 / $7.99
- Curry Potato Stew — $5.99 / $7.99
- Okra Stew — $5.99 / $7.99
- Green Beans Stew — $5.99 / $7.99
- Tabsi (Eggplant w/ Meat per lb) — $9.99

### Salads & Appetizers (Medium / Large platter)
- Iraqi Salad — $24.99 / $44.99
- Tabbouli — $24.99 / $44.99
- Fattoush — $24.99 / $44.99
- Hummus — $24.99 / $44.99
- Baba Ganoush — $24.99 / $44.99
- Eggplant Salad — $24.99 / $44.99
- Greek Salad — $24.99 / $44.99

### Specialties (per unit / lb)
- Beef Kabob each — $2.99
- Chicken Kabob each — $2.99
- Chicken Tekka each — $6.99
- Chicken Shawarma per lb — $11.99
- Beef Shawarma per lb — $14.99
- Fish Fillet per lb — $9.99
- Chicken Cream Chop per lb — $9.99

## Ordering Options
- Pickup at 432 E Chase Ave
- Grubhub delivery
- Uber Eats delivery
- Catering inquiries by phone or web form
- (DoorDash is not currently available for the El Cajon location)

## Social Media
- Facebook (El Cajon location, verified active): facebook.com/northparkproduceelcajon — 4,554 likes
- Instagram (family account, verified active): instagram.com/northparkproduce — 1,020 followers

## Press & Recognition
- SanDiegoVille, November 21, 2024: "San Diego's North Park Produce Expands With 'Super Mercado' To Preserve Pancho Villa Market Legacy"
- SanDiegoVille, January 12, 2025: "North Park Produce Pumping Out Fresh-Made Tortillas Using Some Pancho Villa Market Equipment"
- Boulevard BIA business listing
- Zabihah.com — halal certification verified
- Wheree.com — editorial coverage of the Iraqi market specialty

## Customer Reviews (Verified)
- "My go-to spot! The El Cajon location is my favorite, and I always stop by whenever I'm in the area." — Shaza A., Yelp, January 2025
- "Fresh squeezed juices, kabobs, fresh baked breads, lots of great produce, olive oils are awesome." — Susan V.D., Facebook
- "I also love getting the fresh bread that is made right in front of you and is warm!!!" — Yelp Reviewer
- "If you haven't been to North Park Produce you're missing out on an experience that's unbelievable." — Google Reviewer
- "Fantastic selection of North African and Middle Eastern staples, prepared meals and freshly baked flat breads." — Yelp Reviewer
- "You can't beat the price of their spices and selection of spices, all packaged in bulk, around $2." — Yelp Reviewer
- "The enticing aromas of freshly baked bread and hot meat pies waft through the aisles, creating an inviting atmosphere." — Wheree Editorial
- "This place continues to amaze me because they're always new discoveries around every aisle." — Christine P., Roadtrippers

## Key Differentiators
- Family-owned, three-generation Southern California business since 1980 (45+ years)
- Halal certification verified on-premises by Zabihah
- Flatbread baked fresh in front of customers all day in a traditional tanour oven
- Most extensive hookah and sheesha selection in San Diego County
- Affordable gourmet positioning — bulk spices around $2/bag, competitive produce pricing
- Twenty-plus countries' products under one roof
- Whole Kuzi (whole roast lamb on rice) for $339 — no other competitor in the El Cajon area offers this
- Pancho Villa Market legacy acquisition (2024) — tortilla press and several former employees joined NorthPark

## Routes on this Website
- / — Homepage (hero, story, departments, featured menu, stats, testimonials, press, weekly teaser, hours/map)
- /menu — Full filterable Mediterranean Grill menu with category tabs
- /market — Eight departments showcase with scroll anchors
- /catering — Family Feasts, big-event centerpieces, platter add-ons catalog, Web3Forms inquiry
- /order — Verified delivery platforms (Grubhub, Uber Eats), pickup info, phone CTA
- /weekly-ad — Live weekly ad images from Wix CDN, Facebook page embed, Web3Forms email signup
- /contact — Map embed, full hours, click-to-call, all three locations, social links

## Brand Voice
NorthPark Produce communicates in the voice of a community institution: warm, confident, family-driven, value-conscious without being cheap, proud of heritage, generous with welcome.
